description_long: This object is a realistic yellow flower with six broad, slightly curved petals radiating symmetrically from a central green ovary. The petals are a soft yellow with subtle gradients and naturalistic shading, giving them a lifelike appearance. At the center, several elongated green filaments topped with pinkish anthers extend upward, forming the flower's stamens. The flower is supported by a long, slender green stem that appears smooth and cylindrical. No leaves are visible on or near the stem, emphasizing the flower's solitary presentation. The overall form and coloration suggest a lily or similar ornamental flower commonly found in urban parks or landscaped areas.
description: A yellow flower with six broad petals, a green central ovary, and pink-tipped stamens on a slender green stem. The petals are symmetrically arranged and the flower lacks visible leaves.
description_view_0: Front view showing the symmetrical arrangement of six yellow petals and prominent central stamens.
description_view_1: Slightly rotated view highlighting the curvature of the petals and the central green ovary.
description_view_2: Side view emphasizing the length of the petals and the upward extension of the stamens.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the back of the petals and the attachment to the green stem.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a radial, whorled configuration with six distinct petals symmetrically spaced around the central ovary. Each petal slightly overlaps its neighbor, creating a star-like appearance typical of many monocot flowers.
